BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

Tools/rbk/vov_veiled/FUTURE/RBSPV-PodmanVmSupplyChain.adoc:17:VM images are OCI artifacts rather than standard container images. They contain compressed root filesystem tarballs for WSL environments or CoreOS disk images for standard virtualization platforms. These artifacts use non-standard media types, rely on custom annotations such as disktype to indicate their intended use, and carry opaque blobs rather than composable filesystem layers. While they are platform-indexed in the OCI sense, they are not platform-runnable as containers would be. Because of these differences, standard `podman pull` and `docker pull` cannot correctly retrieve and reassemble VM artifacts — they operate at the container-runtime abstraction level, which assumes runnable images with standard layer structure. Tools like crane (manifest and blob-level registry operations), skopeo (cross-registry copying with format awareness), and oras (OCI Registry As Storage, designed for non-container artifacts) work below that abstraction at the registry API and manifest level, which is why they can handle VM artifacts correctly.

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 03: Conclave the build-tool cohort into a Lode (capture) via docker
3	# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (Google-hosted, always pullable — conclave
4	#          captures the reliquary tools themselves, so it cannot bootstrap from a
5	#          reliquary; the cloud-builders/docker credential helper authenticates
6	#          the GAR push ambiently via the Mason SA, no explicit login)
7	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
8	#                _RBGL_TAG_SPRUE, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
9	#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY
10	#
11	# Pull each build-tool image from upstream, tag it into ONE GAR package
12	# rbi_ld/<stamp> under the clean member tag :rbi_<tool>, and push. Author the
13	# batch provenance envelope (members[] one per tool — the cardinality axis) and
14	# stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for the host capture-file via
15	# /builder/outputs/output. Single-platform (linux/amd64) — tool images run as GCB
16	# steps on amd64 workers, so docker pull/tag/push is sufficient.
17	#
18	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
19	# Member tags on that package, each a distinct tool manifest:
20	#   :<TAG_SPRUE><tool>   e.g. rbi_gcloud, rbi_skopeo   (clean scheme — no digest layer)
21	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
22	
23	set -euo pipefail
24	echo "=== Conclave build-tool cohort into a Lode ==="
25	
26	STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
27	test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }
28	
29	PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
30	echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"
31	
32	# Tool image cohort: short-name|upstream-ref. Authoritative co-versioned set for
33	# GCB step execution — mirrors the legacy inscribe manifest (rbgji01) verbatim;
34	# the two coexist during the reliquary cutover. gcloud and docker are Google-
35	# hosted (gcr.io); the rest are third-party.
36	MANIFEST=(
37	  "gcloud|gcr.io/cloud-builders/gcloud:latest"
38	  "docker|gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker:latest"
39	  "alpine|docker.io/library/alpine:latest"
40	  "syft|docker.io/anchore/syft:latest"
41	  "binfmt|docker.io/tonistiigi/binfmt:latest"
42	  "skopeo|quay.io/skopeo/stable:latest"
43	)
44	
45	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole cohort.
46	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
47	
48	# Accumulate the envelope members[] as we capture (one element per tool). No jq
49	# dependency — values are controlled (tool name, upstream ref, hex digest, SA
50	# email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
51	MEMBERS=''
52	MFIRST=true
53	
54	for ENTRY in "${MANIFEST[@]}"; do
55	  NAME="${ENTRY%%|*}"
56	  UPSTREAM="${ENTRY#*|}"
57	  MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_SPRUE}${NAME}"
58	  DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
59	
60	  echo "--- ${NAME}: ${UPSTREAM} -> ${DEST} ---"
61	
62	  docker pull "${UPSTREAM}" \
63	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to pull ${UPSTREAM}" >&2; exit 1; }
64	
65	  # Record the pulled digest from RepoDigests (format: repo@sha256:...).
66	  DIGEST=$(docker inspect --format='{{index .RepoDigests 0}}' "${UPSTREAM}" 2>/dev/null) || DIGEST=""
67	  DIGEST="${DIGEST##*@}"
68	
69	  docker tag "${UPSTREAM}" "${DEST}" \
70	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to tag ${NAME}" >&2; exit 1; }
71	  docker push "${DEST}" \
72	    || { echo "FATAL: Failed to push ${NAME} to GAR" >&2; exit 1; }
73	
74	  echo "${NAME} captured: ${DEST} (${DIGEST})"
75	
76	  if [ "${MFIRST}" = "true" ]; then MFIRST=false; else MEMBERS="${MEMBERS},"; fi
77	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}{"
78	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
79	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"origin\":\"${UPSTREAM}\","
80	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"digest\":\"${DIGEST}\","
81	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
82	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}\"tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
83	  MEMBERS="${MEMBERS}}"
84	done
85	
86	# Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and
87	# the host capture-file). members[] is the cardinality axis — N for the reliquary
88	# cohort, where bole carries 1.
89	ENVELOPE='{'
90	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
91	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"reliquary\","
92	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
93	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
94	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
95	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
96	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
97	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
98	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[${MEMBERS}]}"
99	
100	# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
101	# stamps file is the step-02 contract; conclave produces exactly one Lode.
102	printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
103	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
104	echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
105	
106	# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
107	# conclave produces exactly one Lode (the cohort is one package).
108	RESULT="{\"slot_1\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"
109	
110	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
111	echo "${RESULT}"
112	
113	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
114	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
115	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
116	
117	echo "=== Conclave capture step complete ==="
118

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 02: Push each Lode's provenance envelope as its :rbi_vouch artifact
3	# Builder: docker (from reliquary)
4	# Entrypoint: bash
5	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH
6	#
7	# Note: The Dockerfile echo-sequence below is intentional — this script runs
8	# inside a Cloud Build container, not under BCG module discipline.
9	#
10	# Step 01 (skopeo) staged one envelope per captured Lode at
11	# /workspace/lode_<stamp>_vouch.json and listed the stamps in
12	# /workspace/lode_stamps.txt. Each envelope rides into the SAME package as the
13	# base manifest, under the :rbi_vouch tag — a distinct manifest, one per Lode.
14	# Vouch content is architecture-independent; single-platform is sufficient.
15	
16	set -euo pipefail
17	
18	echo "=== Assemble and push Lode vouch artifacts ==="
19	
20	test -f /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
21	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt not found — step 01 must run first" >&2; exit 1; }
22	test -s /workspace/lode_stamps.txt \
23	  || { echo "FATAL: /workspace/lode_stamps.txt is empty — nothing ensconced" >&2; exit 1; }
24	
25	test -n "${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_TAG_VOUCH missing" >&2; exit 1; }
26	
27	# Ensure the shared buildx builder — shared library snippet (run once).
28	#@rbgjs_include buildx-bootstrap
29	
30	while IFS= read -r STAMP || test -n "${STAMP}"; do
31	  test -n "${STAMP}" || continue
32	
33	  ENVELOPE_FILE="/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
34	  test -f "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" \
35	    || { echo "FATAL: envelope not staged for ${STAMP}: ${ENVELOPE_FILE}" >&2; exit 1; }
36	
37	  VOUCH_URI="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}:${_RBGL_TAG_VOUCH}"
38	  echo "--- Vouch for ${STAMP} -> ${VOUCH_URI} ---"
39	
40	  CTX="/workspace/vouch_ctx_${STAMP}"
41	  mkdir -p "${CTX}"
42	  cp "${ENVELOPE_FILE}" "${CTX}/vouch.json"
43	  echo "FROM scratch"        >  "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
44	  echo "COPY vouch.json /"   >> "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
45	
46	  # Push the FROM-scratch vouch context — shared library snippet.
47	  PUSH_URI="${VOUCH_URI}"
48	  PUSH_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64"
49	  PUSH_CTX="${CTX}"
50	#@rbgjs_include buildx-push
51	
52	  echo "Vouch pushed: ${VOUCH_URI}"
53	done < /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
54	
55	echo "=== Vouch push step complete ==="
56

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 04: Underpin a WSL rootfs into a Lode (capture) via curl + gpg + buildx
3	# Builder: gcr.io/cloud-builders/docker (Google-hosted, always pullable; Debian-
4	#          based — carries curl + docker buildx, and gnupg is apt-installed by the
5	#          gpg-verify-sums snippet if absent. The underpin acquisition tool is curl
6	#          over HTTPS, not a registry pull, so this kind needs neither skopeo nor a
7	#          reliquary bootstrap. The docker credential helper authenticates the GAR
8	#          push ambiently via the Mason SA.)
9	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT, _RBGL_LODE_STAMP,
10	#                _RBGL_TAG_ROOTFS, _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA,
11	#                _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY, _RBGL_WSL_URL, _RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR
12	#
13	# Fetch the vendor rootfs tarball (URL assembled host-side from the version args)
14	# over HTTPS, DISCOVER its checksum from Canonical's published, GPG-signed
15	# SHA256SUMS (verified against the pinned signing-key fingerprint — the
16	# verified-against-published gate), verify the rootfs bytes against it, then wrap
17	# the OPAQUE tarball as a single-layer OCI member (FROM scratch + COPY — never ADD,
18	# which would extract) and push it into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp> under the
19	# clean member tag :rbi_rootfs. Author the batch provenance envelope (members[]
20	# length 1 — the cardinality axis) and stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch
21	# artifact) and for the host capture-file via /builder/outputs/output.
22	# Single-platform (linux/amd64): the rootfs is an opaque blob, not a multi-arch
23	# manifest. No digest is pinned here — the published checksum is discovered and
24	# signature-verified at capture, per the no-FQIN premise (RBSLU).
25	#
26	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>     (one package = one Lode)
27	# Member tag on that package (clean scheme — no digest/fingerprint layer):
28	#   :<TAG_ROOTFS>   e.g. rbi_rootfs   (the opaque rootfs blob)
29	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
30	#
31	# Trust grade verified-against-published: the member digest recorded in the
32	# envelope is the tarball's GPG-verified published SHA-256, tagged verification
33	# "gpg-sha256-published" with the signing-key fingerprint — NOT an OCI manifest
34	# digest, because what is attested is capture-fidelity of an opaque blob under the
35	# vendor's signed checksum.
36	
37	set -euo pipefail
38	echo "=== Underpin WSL rootfs into a Lode ==="
39	
40	STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_STAMP}"
41	test -n "${STAMP}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_LODE_STAMP missing" >&2; exit 1; }
42	
43	URL="${_RBGL_WSL_URL}"
44	KEY_FPR="${_RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR}"
45	test -n "${URL}"     || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_WSL_URL missing" >&2; exit 1; }
46	test -n "${KEY_FPR}" || { echo "FATAL: _RBGL_WSL_KEY_FPR missing" >&2; exit 1; }
47	
48	PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
49	MEMBER_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_ROOTFS}"
50	DEST="${PKG}:${MEMBER_TAG}"
51	echo "Lode package: ${PKG}"
52	echo "Source:       ${URL}"
53	
54	# Acquisition moment, attested for the single-member cohort.
55	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
56	
57	# --- Discover the published checksum under the pinned signing key ---
58	# The sums + signature live beside the tarball; the target filename is its
59	# basename. The snippet GPG-verifies the sums and provides EXPECTED_SHA.
60	SUMS_URL="${URL%/*}/SHA256SUMS"
61	SIG_URL="${SUMS_URL}.gpg"
62	TARGET_BASENAME="${URL##*/}"
63	#@rbgjs_include gpg-verify-sums
64	
65	# --- Fetch the rootfs over HTTPS (cloud-side; the workstation never touches these
66	# bytes — only the URL was assembled host-side) ---
67	ROOTFS="/workspace/underpin_${STAMP}.rootfs.tar"
68	echo "--- Fetching rootfs tarball ---"
69	curl -fSL --retry 3 -o "${ROOTFS}" "${URL}" \
70	  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to fetch rootfs from ${URL}" >&2; exit 1; }
71	
72	# --- Verify the rootfs bytes against the GPG-verified published checksum ---
73	# Tool availability on the docker builder is a Palisade: sha256sum (coreutils) is
74	# the expected path; shasum / openssl are honest fallbacks so a missing tool fails
75	# the compare loud rather than skipping verification.
76	echo "--- Verifying rootfs against published checksum ---"
77	if command -v sha256sum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
78	  ACTUAL_SHA=$(sha256sum "${ROOTFS}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
79	elif command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
80	  ACTUAL_SHA=$(shasum -a 256 "${ROOTFS}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
81	elif command -v openssl >/dev/null 2>&1; then
82	  ACTUAL_SHA=$(openssl dgst -sha256 "${ROOTFS}" | awk '{print $NF}')
83	else
84	  echo "FATAL: no sha256 tool (sha256sum/shasum/openssl) on builder — cannot verify" >&2
85	  exit 1
86	fi
87	test -n "${ACTUAL_SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: empty digest computed for rootfs" >&2; exit 1; }
88	
89	if [ "${ACTUAL_SHA}" != "${EXPECTED_SHA}" ]; then
90	  echo "FATAL: rootfs checksum mismatch — refusing to capture unverified bytes" >&2
91	  echo "  published (GPG-verified) sha256: ${EXPECTED_SHA}" >&2
92	  echo "  fetched   sha256:                ${ACTUAL_SHA}" >&2
93	  exit 1
94	fi
95	echo "Verified: sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA} (matches GPG-signed published checksum)"
96	
97	# --- Wrap the OPAQUE tarball as a single-layer OCI member and push ---
98	# FROM scratch + COPY (never ADD): COPY stores the tarball byte-for-byte; ADD would
99	# auto-extract and destroy the opaque blob. The shared buildx builder pushes it.
100	echo "--- Wrapping rootfs as OCI member -> ${DEST} ---"
101	CTX="/workspace/underpin_ctx_${STAMP}"
102	mkdir -p "${CTX}"
103	cp "${ROOTFS}" "${CTX}/rootfs.tar"
104	echo "FROM scratch"      >  "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
105	echo "COPY rootfs.tar /" >> "${CTX}/Dockerfile"
106	
107	# Ensure the shared buildx builder — shared library snippet (run once).
108	#@rbgjs_include buildx-bootstrap
109	
110	# Push the FROM-scratch opaque-blob context — shared library snippet.
111	PUSH_URI="${DEST}"
112	PUSH_PLATFORMS="linux/amd64"
113	PUSH_CTX="${CTX}"
114	#@rbgjs_include buildx-push
115	
116	echo "Rootfs captured: ${DEST} (sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA})"
117	
118	# --- Author the batch provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch
119	# and the host capture-file). members[] length 1 — the wsl singleton. No jq
120	# dependency — values are controlled (member tag, URL, hex digest, key fingerprint,
121	# SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote. ---
122	ENVELOPE='{'
123	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
124	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"wsl\","
125	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
126	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
127	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
128	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
129	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
130	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
131	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
132	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${MEMBER_TAG}\","
133	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${URL}\","
134	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${ACTUAL_SHA}\","
135	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"gpg-sha256-published\","
136	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signing_key\":\"${KEY_FPR}\","
137	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${MEMBER_TAG}\"]"
138	ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"
139	
140	# Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact). The
141	# stamps file is the step-02 contract; underpin produces exactly one Lode.
142	printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
143	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
144	echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
145	
146	# Host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope). One slot —
147	# underpin produces exactly one Lode.
148	RESULT="{\"slot_1\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}}"
149	
150	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
151	echo "${RESULT}"
152	
153	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts the touchmark -> capture-file).
154	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
155	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
156	
157	echo "=== Underpin capture step complete ==="
158

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJL Step 01: Ensconce a base image into a Lode (capture) via gcrane
3	# Builder: gcr.io/go-containerregistry/gcrane:debug (Google-hosted, ambient GAR auth)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGL_GAR_HOST, _RBGL_GAR_PATH, _RBGL_LODES_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGL_TAG_BOLE, _RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX,
6	#                _RBGL_TRUST_GRADE, _RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA, _RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY,
7	#                _RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN, _RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN,
8	#                _RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP,   _RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP
9	#
10	# For each non-empty (ORIGIN, STAMP) slot: read the upstream manifest, measure the
11	# canonical digest, gcrane cp into ONE GAR package rbi_ld/<stamp>, then apply the
12	# member tags by gcrane tag (registry-side, no blob round-trip). Author the
13	# provenance envelope and stage it for step 02 (the :rbi_vouch artifact) and for
14	# the host capture-file via /builder/outputs/output. gcrane authenticates GAR
15	# ambiently via its google.Keychain (Mason SA, GCE metadata server) — no token.
16	#
17	# Package shape:  <host>/<path>/<LODES_ROOT>/<stamp>            (one package = one Lode)
18	# Member tags on that package, all pointing at the base manifest:
19	#   :<TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX><full-hex>   canonical OCI digest (exact cross-Lode dedup)
20	#   :<TAG_BOLE>                      uniform greppable handle
21	#   :<sanitized-origin>-<sha10>      UNSPRUED — name + glance-fingerprint (= legacy enshrine anchor)
22	# The :rbi_vouch tag is a separate manifest pushed by step 02.
23	
24	set -euo pipefail
25	echo "=== Ensconce base images into Lodes ==="
26	
27	# No token fetch: gcrane authenticates GAR ambiently through its google.Keychain
28	# (ADC -> GCE metadata server, the Mason SA). No --creds on any gcrane command.
29	
30	# CB substitutions are expanded at submit time, not available as shell variables.
31	# Capture each into a runtime variable so we can loop.
32	SLOT_1_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN}"
33	SLOT_2_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_2_ORIGIN}"
34	SLOT_3_ORIGIN="${_RBGL_IMAGE_3_ORIGIN}"
35	SLOT_1_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_1_STAMP}"
36	SLOT_2_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_2_STAMP}"
37	SLOT_3_STAMP="${_RBGL_LODE_3_STAMP}"
38	
39	# Acquisition moment, attested once for the whole build.
40	ACQUIRED_AT=$(date -u +%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ)
41	
42	# Stamp roster for step 02 (one line per captured Lode).
43	: > /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
44	
45	# Initialize result JSON (host reads this back as the capture-file source)
46	RESULT='{'
47	FIRST=true
48	
49	for SLOT in 1 2 3; do
50	  case "${SLOT}" in
51	    1) ORIGIN="${SLOT_1_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_1_STAMP}" ;;
52	    2) ORIGIN="${SLOT_2_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_2_STAMP}" ;;
53	    3) ORIGIN="${SLOT_3_ORIGIN}"; STAMP="${SLOT_3_STAMP}" ;;
54	  esac
55	  test -n "${ORIGIN}" || continue
56	  test -n "${STAMP}"  || { echo "FATAL: slot ${SLOT} has ORIGIN but no STAMP" >&2; exit 1; }
57	
58	  echo "--- Slot ${SLOT}: ${ORIGIN} -> ${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP} ---"
59	
60	  # Read the upstream manifest, take the canonical digest, derive the glance
61	  # fingerprint — shared snippet (requires ORIGIN + RAW_FILE; provides SHA + FINGERPRINT).
62	  RAW_FILE="/workspace/ensconce_raw_${SLOT}.json"
63	#@rbgjs_include gcrane-fingerprint
64	
65	  DIGEST_TAG="${_RBGL_TAG_DIGEST_PREFIX}${SHA}"
66	  PKG="${_RBGL_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGL_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGL_LODES_ROOT}/${STAMP}"
67	
68	  echo "Package: ${PKG}"
69	  echo "Digest:  sha256:${SHA}"
70	  echo "Tags:    ${DIGEST_TAG}, ${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}, ${FINGERPRINT}"
71	
72	  # Collision guard — never silently clobber an existing Lode. The bole handle
73	  # (:rbi_bole) always points at this touchmark's captured image, so read it:
74	  # absent => a fresh touchmark; present => re-use. Re-use is legitimate ONLY when
75	  # it is the identical canonical digest (a Cloud Build retry re-copying the same
76	  # bytes); a different digest under the same touchmark is a clobber and fails loud.
77	  # The check sits immediately before the copy in this same step — atomic with the
78	  # GAR write in the sense the spec requires (no host pre-submit window). EXISTING_SHA
79	  # is computed the same way as SHA (sha256sum of the raw manifest), so the compare is
80	  # apples-to-apples. A manifest-read failure is treated as absent: cloud-side
81	  # auth/network is reliable, and a genuine GAR outage fails the copy below regardless.
82	  EXISTING_RAW="/workspace/ensconce_existing_${SLOT}.json"
83	  if gcrane manifest "${PKG}:${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" > "${EXISTING_RAW}" 2>/dev/null; then
84	    EXISTING_SHA=$(sha256sum "${EXISTING_RAW}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
85	    if [ "${EXISTING_SHA}" = "${SHA}" ]; then
86	      echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} already holds identical digest sha256:${SHA} — idempotent retry, proceeding."
87	    else
88	      echo "FATAL: touchmark collision at ${PKG}" >&2
89	      echo "  existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE} -> sha256:${EXISTING_SHA}" >&2
90	      echo "  refusing to clobber with  -> sha256:${SHA}" >&2
91	      echo "  (banish the Lode first, or ensconce under a fresh touchmark)" >&2
92	      exit 1
93	    fi
94	  else
95	    echo "Touchmark ${STAMP} is fresh (no existing :${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE})."
96	  fi
97	
98	  # Copy upstream into the Lode package under the canonical digest tag. gcrane cp
99	  # copies the whole reference (manifest list and all per-platform manifests for a
100	  # multi-arch index) and preserves the manifest digest byte-for-byte.
101	  gcrane cp "${ORIGIN}" "${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" \
102	    || { echo "FATAL: gcrane cp failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
103	
104	  # Apply remaining member tags with gcrane tag — a registry-side re-tag of the
105	  # already-copied manifest, no blob round-trip (replaces the GAR->GAR copy).
106	  for MEMBER_TAG in "${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}" "${FINGERPRINT}"; do
107	    gcrane tag "${PKG}:${DIGEST_TAG}" "${MEMBER_TAG}" \
108	      || { echo "FATAL: gcrane tag ${MEMBER_TAG} failed for slot ${SLOT}" >&2; exit 1; }
109	  done
110	
111	  echo "Slot ${SLOT} ensconced: ${STAMP}"
112	
113	  # Author the provenance envelope (identical content lands in :rbi_vouch and the
114	  # host capture-file). No jq dependency — values are controlled (sanitized origin,
115	  # hex digest, SA email, build id, ISO timestamp); none can carry a literal quote.
116	  # members[] is the cardinality axis — length 1 for the bole singleton.
117	  ENVELOPE='{'
118	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"schema\":\"${_RBGL_VOUCH_SCHEMA}\","
119	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"kind\":\"bole\","
120	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"lode\":\"${STAMP}\","
121	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_at\":\"${ACQUIRED_AT}\","
122	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"acquired_by\":\"${_RBGL_ACQUIRED_BY}\","
123	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"capture_build\":\"${BUILD_ID:-}\","
124	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"trust_grade\":\"${_RBGL_TRUST_GRADE}\","
125	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"signature\":null,"
126	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"members\":[{"
127	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"name\":\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\","
128	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"origin\":\"${ORIGIN}\","
129	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"digest\":\"sha256:${SHA}\","
130	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"verification\":\"oci-digest\","
131	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}\"tags\":[\"${_RBGL_TAG_BOLE}\",\"${DIGEST_TAG}\",\"${FINGERPRINT}\"]"
132	  ENVELOPE="${ENVELOPE}}]}"
133	
134	  # Stage the envelope for step 02 (pushes it as the :rbi_vouch artifact).
135	  printf '%s' "${ENVELOPE}" > "/workspace/lode_${STAMP}_vouch.json"
136	  echo "${STAMP}" >> /workspace/lode_stamps.txt
137	
138	  # Accumulate host-facing result (the capture-file carries the same envelope).
139	  if [ "${FIRST}" = "true" ]; then
140	    FIRST=false
141	  else
142	    RESULT="${RESULT},"
143	  fi
144	  RESULT="${RESULT}\"slot_${SLOT}\":{\"stamp\":\"${STAMP}\",\"vouch\":${ENVELOPE}}"
145	done
146	
147	RESULT="${RESULT}}"
148	
149	echo "=== Writing capture results ==="
150	echo "${RESULT}"
151	
152	# Write to buildStepOutputs channel (host extracts per-Lode envelope -> capture-file)
153	mkdir -p /builder/outputs
154	printf '%s' "${RESULT}" > /builder/outputs/output
155	
156	echo "=== Ensconce capture step complete ==="
157

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJS buildx-bootstrap — ensure the shared docker-container buildx builder
3	# exists and is selected. Idempotent under Cloud Build retry: inspect-or-create,
4	# then use. Run once per step before any push; safe to re-run (a second inspect
5	# succeeds, create is skipped, use is a no-op).
6	#   requires: (none)
7	#   provides: the "rb-builder" buildx builder, created if absent and selected
8	docker buildx inspect rb-builder >/dev/null 2>&1 \
9	  || docker buildx create --driver docker-container --name rb-builder
10	docker buildx use rb-builder
11

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJS buildx-push — push a prepared FROM-scratch build context as an image via
3	# the shared buildx builder (see buildx-bootstrap). The irreducible push shared
4	# by the FROM-scratch vouch-push callers (Lode rbgjl02, hallmark-verify rbgjv03):
5	# push cardinality, the destination URI, the platform set, and the context
6	# assembly (which JSON, conditional Dockerfile) are all done by the kind.
7	#   requires: PUSH_URI        full destination image ref including tag
8	#             PUSH_PLATFORMS  buildx --platform value (e.g. linux/amd64)
9	#             PUSH_CTX        build context dir holding the Dockerfile
10	#   provides: the image pushed to PUSH_URI
11	docker buildx build \
12	  --push \
13	  --platform="${PUSH_PLATFORMS}" \
14	  --tag "${PUSH_URI}" \
15	  "${PUSH_CTX}" \
16	  || { echo "FATAL: buildx push failed for ${PUSH_URI}" >&2; exit 1; }
17

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJS gcrane-fingerprint — read an upstream image's raw manifest, take its
3	# canonical sha256 digest, and derive the sanitized-origin glance fingerprint.
4	# The canonical digest is the sha256 of the RAW manifest bytes (what every tool
5	# reports); the fingerprint is the sanitized origin (`:` and `/` become `-`) plus
6	# the first 10 hex of that digest — the legacy enshrine anchor form. `gcrane
7	# manifest` streams the registry's stored manifest bytes verbatim (no
8	# re-serialization), so the digest is byte-identical to skopeo's `inspect --raw`
9	# and to the canonical OCI digest. The first GAR copy is kind-specific (its
10	# destination tag is itself digest-derived) and stays in the step.
11	#   requires: ORIGIN    upstream image ref (e.g. docker.io/library/x:tag)
12	#             RAW_FILE  /workspace path to write the raw manifest into
13	#   provides: SHA          64-hex canonical digest (no algorithm prefix)
14	#             FINGERPRINT  "<sanitized-origin>-<first-10-hex>"
15	gcrane manifest "${ORIGIN}" > "${RAW_FILE}" \
16	  || { echo "FATAL: Failed to read upstream manifest: ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
17	
18	# sha256sum (busybox coreutils, not openssl) — runs inside the gcrane:debug builder.
19	SHA=$(sha256sum "${RAW_FILE}" | cut -d' ' -f1)
20	test -n "${SHA}" || { echo "FATAL: Empty digest for ${ORIGIN}" >&2; exit 1; }
21	
22	# Sanitize origin (: and / become -), append first 10 hex chars (legacy anchor form).
23	FINGERPRINT="$(printf '%s' "${ORIGIN}" | tr ':/' '--')-${SHA:0:10}"
24

1	#!/bin/bash
2	# RBGJM Step 01: Mirror image from upstream to GAR via skopeo
3	# Builder: skopeo (from reliquary)
4	# Substitutions: _RBGA_GAR_HOST, _RBGA_GAR_PATH, _RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT,
5	#                _RBGA_HALLMARK, _RBGA_BIND_SOURCE
6	#
7	# Registry-to-registry copy preserving multi-platform manifest lists.
8	# Mason SA ambient auth via Cloud Build metadata server for GAR destination.
9	# Public upstream images need no auth.
10	#
11	# Destination uses canonical AAK shape:
12	#   ${gar_host}/${gar_path}/${HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${hallmark}/image:${hallmark}
13	# matching rbgja02 (syft), rbgja04 (about), rbgjv03 (vouch).
14	
15	set -euo pipefail
16	
17	test -n "${_RBGA_GAR_HOST}"        || { echo "_RBGA_GAR_HOST missing"        >&2; exit 1; }
18	test -n "${_RBGA_GAR_PATH}"        || { echo "_RBGA_GAR_PATH missing"        >&2; exit 1; }
19	test -n "${_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT}"  || { echo "_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT missing"  >&2; exit 1; }
20	test -n "${_RBGA_HALLMARK}"        || { echo "_RBGA_HALLMARK missing"        >&2; exit 1; }
21	test -n "${_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE}"     || { echo "_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE missing"     >&2; exit 1; }
22	
23	DEST_REF="${_RBGA_GAR_HOST}/${_RBGA_GAR_PATH}/${_RBGA_HALLMARKS_ROOT}/${_RBGA_HALLMARK}/image:${_RBGA_HALLMARK}"
24	
25	echo "=== Mirroring bind image via skopeo ==="
26	echo "Source: ${_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE}"
27	echo "Dest:   ${DEST_REF}"
28	
29	# Obtain OAuth2 token from Cloud Build metadata server (Mason SA)
30	echo "Fetching OAuth2 token from metadata server"
31	TOKEN_JSON=$(curl -sf -H "Metadata-Flavor: Google" \
32	  "http://metadata.google.internal/computeMetadata/v1/instance/service-accounts/default/token") \
33	  || { echo "Failed to fetch OAuth2 token from metadata server" >&2; exit 1; }
34	
35	# Extract access_token (no jq dependency — use grep+cut)
36	TOKEN=$(printf '%s' "${TOKEN_JSON}" | grep -o '"access_token":"[^"]*"' | cut -d'"' -f4)
37	test -n "${TOKEN}" || { echo "Failed to extract access_token from metadata response" >&2; exit 1; }
38	
39	# Registry-to-registry copy preserving multi-platform manifest
40	skopeo copy --all \
41	  "docker://${_RBGA_BIND_SOURCE}" \
42	  "docker://${DEST_REF}" \
43	  --dest-creds "oauth2accesstoken:${TOKEN}" \
44	  || { echo "skopeo copy failed" >&2; exit 1; }
45	
46	echo "Image mirrored: ${DEST_REF}"
47	echo "=== Mirror step complete ==="

content.text:
I have a complete and verified picture. Let me compile the structured findings.

Summary of what I found in the scoped directories (`Tools/rbk/rbgjl`, `rbgjs`, `rbgjm`, `tt/`):

- **tt/**: zero docker/buildx occurrences (confirmed — 204 files, no matches even case-insensitive).
- **rbgjl01** (bole ensconce): already migrated to gcrane (pace T landed). No docker/buildx invocations remain.
- **rbgjl03** (conclave): live `docker pull/inspect/tag/push` invocations → pace U.
- **rbgjs-buildx-push.sh** + **rbgjs-buildx-bootstrap.sh** (shared snippets): the actual buildx invocations, included by rbgjl02 (vouch) and rbgjl04 (underpin) → pace V.
- **rbgjl03 MANIFEST `docker|...`** and **rbgjl02 builder-image refs**: cohort tool-ref data / builder substrate — not invocations to evict.
- **rbgjm01**: skopeo (pace X), no docker/buildx.

One nuance worth flagging: the buildx invocations physically live in the shared rbgjs snippets, and those same snippets are also included by `rbgjv03-assemble-push-vouch.sh` (the hallmark-verify made-side vouch step, OUT of the capture scope). Pace V's description scopes the eviction to the two capture-side callers (rbgjl02, rbgjl04). If the snippets themselves are rewritten to crane-append, that change reaches rbgjv03 too — outside the capture+mirror scope pace D asserts over. This is a shared-snippet coupling the plan should be aware of, but it is not an orphan within the capture scope.
